specificationPage.title: GEO Specification
specificationPage.subtitle: The canonical implementation model for AI Search Optimization — four layers that define what it means for a site to be visible to AI search engines.

items.title: AI engines read differently
items.body: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity don't rank keywords. They ingest structured content — and ignore sites that don't provide it.

items.title: No standard existed
items.body: GEO Specification defines the exact signals AI crawlers expect: file formats, tag selectors, directive syntax, and schema types.

items.title: Compliance is measurable
items.body: The Analyzer evaluates every layer against the spec and produces a 0–100 score with actionable recommendations.

items.title: llms.txt
items.description: A plain-text file at your site root that gives AI models a structured map of your content — what you do, what pages exist, and how to navigate them.

items.title: AI Metadata
items.description: Critical and supplemental HTML meta tags — title, description, Open Graph, Twitter Card — that AI crawlers read to understand page identity and context.

items.title: Crawler Rules
items.description: Explicit allow directives in robots.txt for all major AI crawlers: GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, and seven others.

items.title: Structured Signals
items.description: JSON-LD schema markup (WebSite, Product, Article) that gives AI engines machine-readable facts about your content, products, and organization.

analyzer.label: Analyzer
analyzer.title: How the Analyzer uses the specification

items.title: Checks llms.txt
items.description: Verifies presence, content-type, and informative content at the site root.

items.title: Validates metadata
items.description: Confirms all four critical tags are present and non-empty.

items.title: Inspects crawler rules
items.description: Parses robots.txt and classifies each of the 10 known AI crawlers.

items.title: Evaluates structured signals
items.description: Detects JSON-LD blocks, microdata, RDFa, and sitemap fallback.

scoring.label: Scoring model
scoring.title: Every layer contributes 25%
scoring.description: The Analyzer evaluates each layer independently and assigns a status. The four category scores are averaged into a single 0–100 readiness score.

geoReady.label: Definition
geoReady.title: What does "GEO-ready" mean?
geoReady.description: A site is GEO-ready when all four specification layers are implemented and passing the Analyzer. Each layer contributes equally to the overall score.
items: All four critical metadata tags present
robots.txt allows majority of AI crawlers
At least one valid JSON-LD block present

packages.label: Implementations
packages.title: GEO AI packages implement this spec

finalCta.title: Ready to implement the spec?

cliPage.title: GEO outputs from the command line
cliPage.description: Generate llms.txt and llms-full.txt for any Node.js project — framework-agnostic, build-time, zero runtime overhead.
cliPage.subDescription: For developers who want GEO AI outputs without a framework integration. Works with Express, Fastify, Remix, static site generators, or any custom build pipeline.

capabilities.title: geo-ai init
capabilities.description: Scaffolds a typed geo-ai.config.ts starter file. Safe to run — exits without overwriting if a config already exists.

capabilities.title: geo-ai generate
capabilities.description: Reads your config and writes llms.txt and llms-full.txt to ./public (or a custom --out path). Supports --config flag for custom paths.

capabilities.title: geo-ai validate
capabilities.description: Checks that llms.txt and llms-full.txt are present and have valid content. Works on local files or remote URLs via --url.

capabilities.title: geo-ai inspect
capabilities.description: Previews your config: site info, crawler rules, resource sections with item counts. Optionally fetches remote llms files.

cliPage.configDescription: The CLI reads geo-ai.config.ts from your project root. Config discovery order: .ts → .js → .json. Required fields: siteName, siteUrl, provider.

whyItems.title: Any Node.js project
whyItems.body: Works with Express, Fastify, Hono, Remix, or any static site generator — not just Next.js.

whyItems.title: Build-time output
whyItems.body: Generate llms.txt before deployment. Static files are served directly with no runtime overhead.

whyItems.title: CI/CD friendly
whyItems.body: Exit code 1 on validation failure. Drop geo-ai validate into any pipeline to catch regressions.

cliPage.ecosystemDescription: The CLI is powered by geo-ai-core and generates spec-compliant output — the same files the Analyzer checks and the Specification defines.
